title: Oral history transcription of Shigeru Hidaka
creator: Issei Oral History Project
description: Please visit the California Revealed website to see the most complete and up-to-date version of this object: https://californiarevealed.org/node/567818 .

English-translated transcriptions of oral history recorded in Japanese as part of the Issei Oral History Project.¶Interview II (undated). Location of interview: Seattle, Washington. Religion: Christian. Interviewer: Takarabe, Rev. Heihachiro.¶Interview No. 76. Part of a group interview with Seattle Presbyterian Issei that includes Shigeru Hidaka, Jitsuo Otoshi, Teiko Tomita, and Kotaka Uomoto. Location of interview: Seattle, Washington. Religion: Christian. Interviewer: Takarabe, Rev. Heihachiro¶The Issei Oral History Project was conducted under the authority of the Board of Governors of the Issei Oral History Project Inc. and the Rev. Heihachiro Takarabe between 1969 and 1980. The project recorded 207 personal interviews of Issei (first generation) Japanese immigrants who lived in Sacramento and other areas of Northern California. In their oral histories, interviewees discuss their lives in Japan, coming to the United States, where they worked and lived in California, their experiences during and after the incarceration of Japanese in the U.S. during World War II, and social and religious activities.
